headline: AMD Q1 2026: Datacenter Revenue Surges 57% to $5.8B, Shares Jump 16%
summary: AMD reported Q1 revenue of $10.3B (+38% YoY) with datacenter segment hitting $5.8B (+57% YoY). Non-GAAP EPS $1.37 beat estimates. Q2 guidance of $11.2B also above consensus. Record free cash flow of $2.6B, tripling year-over-year.

headline: NVIDIA Partners with Corning in Up to $3.2B Optical Fiber Deal for AI Infrastructure
summary: NVIDIA and Corning announced a multiyear partnership to expand US-based manufacturing of optical connectivity for AI infrastructure. Three new factories in NC and TX, 3000+ new jobs. NVIDIA gets warrants to invest up to $3.2B in Corning. Aims to replace copper with optical glass fibers in AI rack-scale systems.

headline: BofA Hikes 2026 Semiconductor Forecast to $1.3 Trillion, AI to Represent 30%
summary: Bank of America raised its 2026 global semiconductor revenue forecast to $1.3 trillion, the highest growth in two decades. AI semiconductors projected at 30% of total revenue. NVIDIA, Broadcom, Marvell, AMD named as top drivers.

headline: Hyperscaler Capex Approaches $830B in 2026, Up 77% Year-Over-Year
summary: Combined capex from Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta projected at $725-830B in 2026. Microsoft set calendar-year 2026 capex at $190B, Alphabet guided up to $190B, Meta raised guidance to $145B. Approximately 75% of aggregate capex funds AI-related infrastructure.

headline: Hut 8 Signs $9.8B, 15-Year AI Datacenter Lease at Beacon Point; Stock Jumps 30%
summary: Hut 8 commercializes first phase of 1 GW Beacon Point AI datacenter campus in Texas with 352 MW IT lease. Base-term contract value $9.8B with options to $25.1B. Built to NVIDIA DSX reference architecture. Expected average annual NOI of $655M upon stabilization. Operations start 2027.

headline: Blackstone Files for $1.75B Datacenter REIT IPO Under 'BXDC' Ticker
summary: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust filed for IPO targeting $1.75B at $20/share. Will focus on acquiring new-construction datacenters utilized by hyperscalers ($250M-$1.5B range). Together with Singapore's DayOne Data Centers, may raise close to $7B in aggregate.

headline: Datacenter Construction Pipeline: 2,913 Facilities Worth $2.4 Trillion Planned Through 2030
summary: Data center developers and Big Tech plan to start construction on 2,913 datacenters valued at approximately $2.4 trillion over 2026-2030. US leads with the largest share of planned capacity.

headline: NANO Nuclear Signs MOU with Supermicro to Power AI Datacenters with Microreactors
summary: NANO Nuclear Energy entered strategic MOU with Supermicro to explore integrating advanced microreactor systems with AI server and datacenter platforms. Part of growing trend of SMR-datacenter partnerships.

headline: GE Vernova Raises 2026 Guidance After Q1 Beat: Datacenter Equipment Orders of $2.4B in One Quarter
summary: GE Vernova posted Q1 orders of $18.3B (+71% YoY). Electrification segment booked $2.4B in datacenter equipment orders, exceeding all of 2025. Electrification orders up 86% YoY. Gas Power has 20% of 100 GW under contract supporting datacenters. Management raised full-year guidance.

headline: Constellation Energy Misses 2026 Guidance After Calpine Acquisition; Nuclear Pipeline Grows to 45 GW
summary: Constellation guided 2026 earnings below analyst expectations, disappointing the market. However, after closing the Calpine acquisition, became the largest private-sector US power producer with 55 GW. Pipeline of conditional datacenter-SMR offtake agreements has grown from 25 GW (end 2024) to 45 GW.

headline: AI Software Sector Repricing: Palantir -19% YTD, ServiceNow -41% YTD as Investors Rotate to Infrastructure
summary: Broad repricing event across AI software stocks as investors rotate from expensive platform stocks to hardware and energy infrastructure. Palantir down 19% YTD despite operating beats; Citi cut target to $210. ServiceNow -41% YTD despite Now Assist tracking toward $1B ACV. Snowflake -33% YTD but trading at more attractive 10x sales.

headline: Vertiv Q1 2026: Revenue +30% YoY, $15B Backlog, Guides EPS 12-14% Above Consensus
summary: Vertiv posted Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.17 vs $1.01 expected on revenue of $2.65B (+30% YoY). Organic orders growth 252% YoY. Backlog swelled to $15B (+109%). FY2026 revenue guidance $13.25-13.75B with adjusted EPS $5.97-6.07, crushing $5.33 consensus.

headline: Coherent Corp Q3 FY2026: Revenue Up 21% to $1.81B on AI Optical Demand
summary: Coherent reported Q3 fiscal 2026 revenue of $1.81B (+21% YoY). GAAP EPS $0.97, non-GAAP EPS $1.41. Strong demand driven by AI datacenter optical transceiver and networking equipment.

headline: Quanta Services Q1 2026: Record Revenue $7.87B, Backlog $48.5B on Datacenter Buildout
summary: Quanta Services posted Q1 adjusted EPS of $2.68 vs $2.03 expected on revenue of $7.87B (+26% YoY). Record backlog of $48.5B. FY2026 revenue guidance $34.7-35.2B. Workforce flexibility across $2.4T addressable market spanning utilities and datacenters.
